content.full_text:
January 6, 1977

The Honorable John Vasconcellos
Member of the Assembly
2435 Forest Avenue
San Jose, California 95129

Dear Assemblyman Vasconcellos:

This letter is in response to a request made by your administrative aide, Mabel Brown, concerning a possible amendment to the Fair Political Practices Commission's regulation 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18727 which establishes a rule for when hospitality involving food, beverages or lodging are reportable on Statements of Economic Interest.

The Commission staff presently is working on the initial stages of a major evaluation project which we anticipate will result in changes in many regulations currently in effect. Regulation Section 18727 will be considered for amendment after that evaluation is completed. As a part of the evaluation project the Commission is requesting reaction to its activities to date and would appreciate receiving your recommendation outlining how you would like to see regulation Section 18727 changed and any other comments you may have.

I, or any member of the Commission or its staff will be happy to discuss your recommendations and comments.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.

sections.question_synthetic: The inquiry concerns whether and how regulation 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18727, which governs the reporting of hospitality involving food, beverages, or lodging on Statements of Economic Interest, should be amended.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC indicates that Regulation Section 18727 will be considered for amendment after the Commission completes an ongoing major evaluation project, and the Commission invites the assemblyman to provide recommendations on desired changes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a response letter but does not contain formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The query was submitted through an administrative aide. The response is transactional in nature, acknowledging a request and indicating future review rather than providing direct legal advice.

QUESTION: The inquiry concerns whether and how regulation 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18727, which governs the reporting of hospitality involving food, beverages, or lodging on Statements of Economic Interest, should be amended.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC indicates that Regulation Section 18727 will be considered for amendment after the Commission completes an ongoing major evaluation project, and the Commission invites the assemblyman to provide recommendations on desired changes.

embedding.summary: FPPC correspondence responding to Assemblyman Vasconcellos regarding a proposed amendment to regulation Section 18727 concerning reportable hospitality. The FPPC indicates the regulation will be reviewed as part of a broader evaluation project and requests the assemblyman's recommendations.
